reasoning: The most advanced leprosy specific vaccine candidate, LepVax, only entered Phase 1/1b clinical trials in late 2024. Vaccine development, through all phases of clinical trials and regulatory approval, takes multiple years. There is virtually no chance a leprosy vaccine will be completed, approved, and correctly recorded as such on Wikipedia by February 2026. The only minimal probability arises from a Wikipedia editor incorrectly adding an existing immunotherapeutic (like MIP or BCG) into the vaccine column during this timeframe, though this column has historically remained empty and such an edit would likely be reverted. Therefore, the probabilities are kept at the minimum allowable thresholds, slightly increasing over time due to baseline epistemic uncertainty.
